text:
There was once a peculiar old owl who lived in a sprawling forest known for its talking birds. This owl had a peculiar talent; every night, after dinner, it could summon any bird it chose to have a conversation.

The owl summoned a starling one evening. "Tell me about your life."

The starling chirped enthusiastically. "Oh, it's wonderful! I get to visit every nest I fancy, nest hop a bit, and enjoy all those nice worms. Then I sleep upside down on the eaves!"

The owl chuckled. "Maybe there's a nest with a talking cat?"

The starling squawked excitedly.

"That'd be splendid!"

The owl pondered deep thoughts late into the night.

One evening, as usual, the owl thought about owlets learning to fly.

"Wouldn't it be exciting?" the owl mused.

The starling chattered eagerly. "Oh yes! They flap beautifully! They feed almost instantly! And they find their own places
